title: St. Mary's Cathedral, Pattom, Trivandrum
tagline: St. Mary's Cathedral, Pattom, Trivandrum — heritage city of Kerala

heroImage.alt: St. Mary's Cathedral, Pattom, Trivandrum, Kerala

overview.short: St. Mary's Cathedral, Pattom, Trivandrum is a much-loved heritage city in Kerala, known for forts and architecture.
overview.description: St. Mary's Malankara Syrian Catholic Cathedral, Pattom, Trivandrum is the central place of worship of the Syro-Malankara Catholic Church. It is the seat of the Major Archbishop of Trivandrum. The tombs of The Venerable Archbishop Geevarghese Mar Ivanios, Archbishop Benedict Mar Gregorios and of Major Archbishop Cyril Baselios Catholicos are located here.

topPlaces.name: Thiruvananthapuram
topPlaces.description: Thiruvananthapuram (Malayalam pronunciation: [t̪iɾuʋənən̪d̪ɐˈbuɾəm] tirr-ROO-və-nun-Tə-poor-əm), also known as Trivandrum (British colonial term still used by many Indians and Indian organisations) is the capital city of the Indian state of Kerala. As of 2011, the Thiruvananthapuram Municipal Corporation had a population of 957,730 over an area of 214.86 km2, making it the most populous city in Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Kendriya Vidyalaya, Pattom
topPlaces.description: Kendriya Vidyalaya Pattom (KV Pattom) is a school in Pattom, Thiruvananthapuram, India. It was established in 1964 as a civil sector school affiliated to CBSE. KV Pattom runs with two shifts from Class I to Class XII, with a roll of 3500+ students and 100+ staff.

topPlaces.name: Thiruvananthapuram Orthodox Diocese
topPlaces.description: Thiruvananthapuram Orthodox Diocese is one of the 32 dioceses of Malankara Orthodox Syrian Church.

topPlaces.name: Sri Padmanabha Theatre
topPlaces.description: Sri Padmanabha Theatre is a cinema theatre in the South Indian city of Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala. Located in Chalai, it was opened in 1936 and is one of the oldest theatres in Kerala.

topPlaces.name: International Film Festival of Kerala
topPlaces.description: The International Film Festival of Kerala (IFFK) is a film festival held annually in Thiruvananthapuram, the capital city of Kerala, India. This film festival started in 1996 and is hosted by the Kerala State Chalachitra Academy on behalf of Department of Cultural Affairs, Government of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Pattom
topPlaces.description: Pattom is a dense commercial region of Thiruvananthapuram city in Kerala, India. It is located approximately 4 kilometres north of centre of Thampanoor.

topPlaces.name: Ulloor
topPlaces.description: Ulloor is a city locality in the Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala, India. It was the home of the modern Malayalam triumvirate poet Ulloor S. Parameswara Iyer.

topPlaces.name: Regional Cancer Centre, Thiruvananthapuram
topPlaces.description: The Regional Cancer Centre (RCC) at Thiruvananthapuram is a cancer care hospital and research centre. It was established in 1981 by the Government of Kerala and the Government of India.

items.note: Thiruvananthapuram (Malayalam pronunciation: [t̪iɾuʋənən̪d̪ɐˈbuɾəm] tirr-ROO-və-nun-Tə-poor-əm), also known as Trivandrum (British colonial term still used by many Indians and Indian organisations) is the capital city of the Indian state of Kerala. As of 2011, the Thiruvananthapuram Municipal Corporation had a population of 957,730 over an area of 214.86 km2, making it the most populous city in Kerala.

Kendriya Vidyalaya Pattom (KV Pattom) is a school in Pattom, Thiruvananthapuram, India. It was established in 1964 as a civil sector school affiliated to CBSE. KV Pattom runs with two shifts from Class I to Class XII, with a roll of 3500+ students and 100+ staff.
Thiruvananthapuram Orthodox Diocese is one of the 32 dioceses of Malankara Orthodox Syrian Church.
Sri Padmanabha Theatre is a cinema theatre in the South Indian city of Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala. Located in Chalai, it was opened in 1936 and is one of the oldest theatres in Kerala.

The International Film Festival of Kerala (IFFK) is a film festival held annually in Thiruvananthapuram, the capital city of Kerala, India. This film festival started in 1996 and is hosted by the Kerala State Chalachitra Academy on behalf of Department of Cultural Affairs, Government of Kerala.
Pattom is a dense commercial region of Thiruvananthapuram city in Kerala, India. It is located approximately 4 kilometres north of centre of Thampanoor.
Ulloor is a city locality in the Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala, India. It was the home of the modern Malayalam triumvirate poet Ulloor S. Parameswara Iyer.
The Regional Cancer Centre (RCC) at Thiruvananthapuram is a cancer care hospital and research centre. It was established in 1981 by the Government of Kerala and the Government of India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit St. Mary's Cathedral, Pattom, Trivandrum?
faq.a: The best time to visit St. Mary's Cathedral, Pattom, Trivandrum is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach St. Mary's Cathedral, Pattom, Trivandrum?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (5 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (4 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for St. Mary's Cathedral, Pattom, Trivandrum?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of St. Mary's Cathedral, Pattom, Trivandrum at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in St. Mary's Cathedral, Pattom, Trivandrum?
faq.a: Stays in St. Mary's Cathedral, Pattom, Trivandrum start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in St. Mary's Cathedral, Pattom, Trivandrum?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thiruvananthapuram, Kendriya Vidyalaya, Pattom, Thiruvananthapuram Orthodox Diocese, Sri Padmanabha Theatre, International Film Festival of Kerala.
